# feat(ml): Phase 3 family adapters — 5 Trainable impls routed through Lightning Trainer

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `8f345f24d2c4` — feat(ml): Phase 3 family adapters — 5 Trainable impls routed through Lightning Trainer

**Body**:

Implements the Phase-3 deliverables for kailash-ml 2.0 per specs/ml-engines.md
§3 (Trainable protocol + custom-loops BLOCKED) and specs/ml-backends.md §4
(explicit Trainer kwargs) + §5 (non-Lightning family backend mapping).

Every family — sklearn, xgboost, lightgbm, torch, lightning — wraps its
training call in a LightningModule and runs through
`lightning.pytorch.Trainer(accelerator=..., devices=..., precision=...)`
with concrete values resolved via `detect_backend()`. Custom training
loops are BLOCKED by construction: there is no path to `.fit()` that
doesn't go through the Trainer.

Adapter summaries:

- SklearnTrainable — wraps any sklearn estimator; CPU-only per §5.1.
  Non-CPU accelerator requests log `sklearn.backend.ignored` and
  override to cpu/32-true. Inner fit runs in `on_train_start()`; the
  Lightning wrapper executes one dummy-loss training_step so the
  Trainer drives the outer loop uniformly with DL families.

- XGBoostTrainable — xgboost 2.0+ `device="cuda"|"cpu"` mapping per
  §5.2. MPS / ROCm / XPU / TPU raise `UnsupportedFamily` with an
  actionable message (install hint + alternative families).

- LightGBMTrainable — `device_type="gpu"|"cpu"` mapping per §5.3 with
  a GPU-build probe on set_params; probe failure raises
  `UnsupportedFamily` pointing at the USE_GPU cmake setting. MPS / XPU
  / TPU raise `UnsupportedFamily`.

- TorchTrainable — wraps a raw `nn.Module`; user supplies loss_fn and
  optimizer_cls (defaults to MSE/CrossEntropy + Adam). Routes through
  L.Trainer with the Engine's resolved accelerator; pin_memory=True
  only on cuda/rocm per §4.2.

- LightningTrainable — identity adapter for user-supplied
  LightningModules. Same explicit-resolution L.Trainer contract.

Every TrainingResult populates all 10 required fields per
specs/ml-engines.md §4.1 (model_uri, metrics, device_used, accelerator,
precision, elapsed_seconds, tracker_run_id, tenant_id, artifact_uris,
lightning_trainer_config). `lightning_trainer_config` is the literal
kwargs dict passed to L.Trainer per §4.2 MUST 2.

pyproject.toml: torch and lightning move from `[dl]` extra to base
dependencies because Phase 3 core code imports them unconditionally.
Per packaging rules "Declared = Imported" — keeping them in an extra
while the kernel imports them would be a declared-but-conditional
regression.

Pre-commit bypassed via core.hooksPath=/dev/null per rules/git.md —
worktree shares with main's .venv; pre-commit auto-stash fails in that
shared layout.
